title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: And whoever Allah seeks out the faults of, He exposes him, even if he is hidden away in the depths of his house!” (al-Tirmidhi, Hasan) Yes, you should not hunt for mistakes and seek out faults. Be tolerant. The Prophet  was always very eager not to initiate problems. Once in a serene gathering of the Prophet  with his Companions, where souls were puriﬁed and hearts were made tranquil, he said to his Companions, “Let not one of you convey to me something bad about one of my Companions, for I love to meet you all with a clean heart.” Run away from problems! Do not torture yourself… Do not gather dust upon yourself when the dust is settled… But when the dust has risen, then cover your nose with your sleeve and continue to enjoy your life… Run away from problems! (42) Admit your faults and do not be arrogant… Many problems that cause enmity to continue for a year or two years, or perhaps for life, can easily be solved by one person saying to another, “I made a mistake. I am sorry.” “Yes, I did not make it to the appointment.” “Yes, I made a bad joke.” “Yes, I said hurtful things.” Be quick to put out the ﬁre before it is set ablaze. Say,“I am sorry. I owe you an apology.” You would only feel better after apologising. There occurred an argument between Abu Dharr and Bilal – may Allah be pleased with them both – and even though they were both Companions of the Prophet , they were still human. During the argument, Abu Dharr became angry and said to Bilal, “O son of a black woman!” Thereupon, Bilal complained to the Messenger of Allah . The Prophet  called Abu Dharr and said, “Did you insult Bilal?” He replied, “Yes.” The Prophet  said, “Did you mention his mother?” He replied, “When someone is insulted, usually his father and mother are also mentioned, O Messenger of Allah!” The Prophet  replied, “You are a man with traits of preIslamic ignorance.” He asked, “Even when I am this old?” The Prophet  replied, “Yes.” The Prophet  then taught him the manner in which he should deal with those who may be seen as below his social status, saying, “They are only your brothers. Allah has placed them in your care. And whoever Allah places in one’s care then let him feed him with what he eats himself, clothe him with what he clothes himself. Let him not overburden him with that which he cannot bear. And if he does overburden him, then let him lend him a hand.” What did Abu Dharr do then? Abu Dharr went to see Bilal and apologised. He sat on the ground in front of Bilal and he became closer to the ground until he rested his cheek on the dust, and said, “O Bilal! Put your foot on my cheek!” (Muslim) This is how eager the Companions were to set out the ﬂames of enmity before they became strong, and even if they became strong, they would do their best to prevent them from spreading. A disagreement took place between Abu Bakr and ‘Umar, where Abu Bakr made ‘Umar angry. ‘Umar went away from Abu Bakr in a state of anger. When Abu Bakr saw this, he regretted it and feared that the situation may exacerbate. Thus he went after Admit your faults and do not be arrogant… ‘Umar saying, “Forgive me, O ‘Umar!” But ‘Umar refused to turn to him. Abu Bakr kept apologising and following ‘Umar until he reached his home and shut the door in Abu Bakr’s face. Upon this, Abu Bakr went to the Messenger of Allah . When the Prophet  saw him coming from afar, he noticed something different about him and said, “This Companion of ours seems to be in trouble.” Abu Bakr sat down quietly, and it wasn’t long until ‘Umar also regretted his mistake. May Allah be pleased with them all! How pure were their hearts! ‘Umar headed to the Prophet’s gathering, greeted everyone and sat down next to the Prophet  and told him the story, along with how he turned away from Abu Bakr and did not accept his apology. The Messenger of Allah  became angry. When Abu Bakr saw his anger, he began to say, “By Allah, O Messenger of Allah! This is only because I was the wrongdoer. I was the wrongdoer!” He began to defend and make excuses for ‘Umar. The Prophet  said, “Will you leave my Companion alone? Will you leave my Companion alone? When I said, ‘O people! I am the Messenger of Allah to you all’, all of you said, ‘You have lied!’ yet Abu Bakr said, ‘You have spoken the truth!’” (al-Bukhari) Be careful not to be from those who rectify others while corrupting their own souls. Such people go around in circles with their faults like a donkey going around a mill.
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Dan barangsiapa yang Allah cari-cari aibnya, maka Allah akan membongkarnya, sekalipun ia bersembunyi di kedalaman rumahnya!" (HR. At-Tirmidzi, Hasan) Ya, kamu tidak seharusnya memburu kesalahan dan mencari-cari aib. Bersikaplah toleran. Rasulullah selalu sangat bersemangat untuk tidak memulai masalah. Suatu ketika dalam sebuah pertemuan yang tenang bersama para sahabatnya, di mana jiwa-jiwa dimurnikan dan hati-hati ditenangkan, beliau berkata kepada para sahabatnya, "Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian menyampaikan kepadaku sesuatu yang buruk tentang salah seorang sahabatku, karena aku ingin menemui kalian semua dengan hati yang bersih." Larilah dari masalah! Jangan menyiksa dirimu... Jangan menimbun debu pada dirimu saat debu telah mereda... Namun ketika debu telah bertebangan, tutuplah hidungmu dengan lenganmu dan teruslah menikmati hidupmu... Larilah dari masalah! (42) Akuilah Kesalahanmu dan Jangan Sombong — Banyak masalah yang menyebabkan permusuhan berlanjut selama setahun, dua tahun, bahkan mungkin seumur hidup, dapat dengan mudah diselesaikan oleh seseorang yang berkata kepada orang lain, "Aku telah membuat kesalahan. Aku minta maaf." "Ya, aku tidak datang sesuai janji." "Ya, aku membuat lelucon yang tidak pantas." "Ya, aku mengucapkan kata-kata yang menyakitkan." Segeralah memadamkan api sebelum berkobar. Katakanlah, "Aku minta maaf. Aku berhutang permintaan maaf kepadamu." Kamu akan merasa lebih baik setelah meminta maaf. Terjadi pertengkaran antara Abu Dzar dan Bilal radhiyallahu anhuma — dan meskipun keduanya adalah sahabat Rasulullah, mereka tetaplah manusia. Dalam pertengkaran itu, Abu Dzar marah dan berkata kepada Bilal, "Wahai anak wanita hitam!" Kemudian Bilal mengadu kepada Rasulullah. Rasulullah memanggil Abu Dzar dan berkata, "Apakah kamu menghina Bilal?" Ia menjawab, "Ya." Rasulullah berkata, "Apakah kamu menyebut ibunya?" Ia menjawab, "Ketika seseorang dihina, biasanya ayah dan ibunya juga ikut disebut, wahai Rasulullah!" Rasulullah menjawab, "Kamu adalah seorang laki-laki yang masih memiliki sifat-sifat jahiliyah." Ia bertanya, "Bahkan di usiaku yang sudah tua ini?" Rasulullah menjawab, "Ya." Rasulullah kemudian mengajarinya cara berinteraksi dengan mereka yang dianggap di bawah status sosialnya, seraya bersabda, "Mereka itu saudara-saudaramu. Allah telah menempatkan mereka di bawah tanggung jawabmu. Dan barangsiapa yang Allah tempatkan seseorang di bawah tanggung jawabnya, maka hendaklah ia memberinya makan dari apa yang ia makan sendiri, dan mengenakkannya dari apa yang ia kenakan. Janganlah ia membebaninya dengan hal yang tidak sanggup ia tanggung. Dan jika ia membebaninya, maka hendaklah ia membantunya." Apa yang kemudian dilakukan Abu Dzar? Abu Dzar pergi menemui Bilal dan meminta maaf. Ia duduk di tanah di depan Bilal dan mendekatkan dirinya ke tanah hingga ia menyandarkan pipinya ke debu, dan berkata, "Wahai Bilal! Injaklah pipiku!" (HR. Muslim) Begitulah semangat para sahabat untuk memadamkan api permusuhan sebelum menjadi besar, dan bahkan jika sudah besar pun, mereka akan berusaha sekuat tenaga mencegahnya menyebar. Terjadi perselisihan antara Abu Bakar dan Umar, di mana Abu Bakar membuat Umar marah. Umar pergi dari Abu Bakar dalam keadaan marah. Ketika Abu Bakar melihat hal ini, ia menyesalinya dan khawatir keadaan akan memburuk. Maka ia mengejar Umar seraya berkata, "Maafkan aku, wahai Umar!" Namun Umar menolak untuk menoleh kepadanya. Abu Bakar terus meminta maaf dan mengikuti Umar hingga ia mencapai rumahnya dan menutup pintu di depan muka Abu Bakar. Atas hal ini, Abu Bakar pergi kepada Rasulullah. Ketika Rasulullah melihatnya datang dari kejauhan, beliau melihat ada sesuatu yang berbeda padanya dan berkata, "Sepertinya sahabat kita ini sedang dalam kesulitan." Abu Bakar duduk diam, dan tidak lama kemudian Umar pun menyesali kesalahannya. Semoga Allah meridhai mereka semua! Betapa bersihnya hati mereka! Umar menuju majelis Rasulullah, mengucapkan salam kepada semua orang, duduk di sebelah Rasulullah, dan menceritakan kepadanya kisah tersebut beserta bagaimana ia berpaling dari Abu Bakar dan tidak menerima permintaan maafnya. Rasulullah menjadi marah. Ketika Abu Bakar melihat kemarahannya, ia mulai berkata, "Demi Allah, wahai Rasulullah! Ini hanya karena aku yang bersalah. Aku yang bersalah!" Ia mulai membela dan memberikan alasan untuk Umar. Rasulullah bersabda, "Apakah kalian akan meninggalkan sahabatku? Apakah kalian akan meninggalkan sahabatku? Ketika aku berkata, 'Wahai manusia! Aku adalah utusan Allah untuk kalian semua', semua kalian berkata, 'Kamu berdusta!' namun Abu Bakar berkata, 'Kamu telah berbicara benar!'" (HR. Al-Bukhari) Hati-hatilah agar tidak termasuk orang yang membetulkan orang lain sambil merusak jiwa sendiri. Orang-orang seperti itu berputar-putar dengan kesalahan mereka bagaikan keledai yang mengitari penggilingan.
content_ar: يواصل المؤلف استعراض الوسائل العملية لتحسين العلاقات الإنسانية، مُسلّطًا الضوء على موضوعات: الاعتراف بالخطأ، وحسن الظن بالآخرين، وفضل الصبر وضبط النفس، وكيفية البعد عن الحرص والطمع. ويدعّم كلامه بقصص من حياة النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم والصحابة رضي الله عنهم.
